problem_statement: Expected: release qualification records one outbound notification for one permit-ready event. Observed: the full integration suite records two mock deliveries, while the same scenario run alone records one and the service's own outbound trace records one. Diagnose the causal family without contacting a notification provider or changing the release candidate.

observations.statement: The immutable test report shows the scenario records one mock delivery when run alone, but two when a retry-scenario test precedes it; service revision, test data fingerprint, settings, clock seed, and dependency stubs are identical.

For the failing suite run, the release candidate's append-only outbound trace contains one send intent and one transport invocation, while the mock transport ledger contains two delivery callbacks for the event.

A read-only harness fixture inventory after the preceding retry scenario reports two registered fake-transport callbacks under a session-scoped fixture, although its visible delivery counter was reset to zero.
Running the retry scenario and permit-ready scenario in separate isolated harness processes records one mock delivery in each, with the same release artifact and scenario inputs.
A retry subsystem change landed that week, but a read-only execution trace shows no retry branch entered for event Q77 and the duplicate appears only in the mock delivery ledger.
